algorithms.title: Psychotic/Mood Disorder and Suicide Risk Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for recognizing and safely managing psychotic symptoms, mood disorder presentations, and suicide risk.
steps: Assess scene safety and check vital signs/glucose to screen for organic/reversible causes when feasible.
For panic-like presentations (chest tightness, dyspnea, tachycardia, fear): perform a proper medical rule-out (ECG, vitals, glucose) rather than assuming panic disorder from history alone.
For psychotic symptoms (delusions, hallucinations): approach calmly, avoid arguing with or challenging the content, and proceed with safe assessment and transport.
For mood symptoms (depressive or manic presentation): assess for safety risks — impaired judgment/risk-taking in mania, suicidal ideation in depression.
Ask directly and non-judgmentally about suicidal ideation when indicated — this does not increase risk.
If suicidal ideation is present with a specific plan, access to lethal means, or prior attempt history: treat as high-risk, apply scene safety considerations, and transport per local protocol.
